action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 22 failed because model.py couldn't import mmseg due to an mmengine Adafactor registration conflict. The checkpoint keys (shown in iter 23 stdout) confirm the architecture is timm ConvNeXt-L + mmseg UPerHead + FCNHead. Best official mIoU is 0.5132 (iter 16, 7-scale TTA with max_size=3200). Remaining time is ~67 min. Analysis: The fastest path to recover the best score is to fix model.py so it loads the existing checkpoint. I will monkey-patch mmengine's Registry._register_module to suppress 'already registered' errors before importing mmseg heads. I will also bump predict.py's max_size back to 3200 (the setting that yielded 0.5132). Then I'll run a 15-second model-load sanity check. If it passes, I'll immediately submit eval to run the full TTA inference (~30-40 min, well within the 67-min budget). Decision: Write patched model.py, update predict.py max_size=3200, run sanity check, submit eval.
action.command:
python -c "
import torch
from model import SegmentationModel
m = SegmentationModel(num_classes=150, aux=True)
ckpt = torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt', map_location='cpu')
sd = ckpt['model'] if isinstance(ckpt, dict) and 'model' in ckpt else ckpt
sd = {k[7:] if k.startswith('module.') else k: v for k, v in sd.items()}
missing, unexpected = m.load_state_dict(sd, strict=False)
print('Missing keys:', len(missing))
print('Unexpected keys:', len(unexpected))
if missing: print(missing[:5])
if unexpected: print(unexpected[:5])
m.eval()
with torch.no_grad():
    y = m(torch.randn(1,3,512,512))
print('Forward OK', y.shape)
"
